normalized: The plan's consent mechanism could be written by the agent itself, so the hook enforced deliberateness rather than actual human consent, failing to deliver the claimed guarantee.

normalized: The plan relied on permissionDecision 'ask' behavior that, per official docs and known bugs, hangs in headless mode, so the guard would deadlock the orchestrator it was built to enable; a mode branch was required.

normalized: A pasted corrections doc required verifying that permissionDecision deny is actually honored for Edit/MultiEdit (a documented bug), removing session-sweep logic that corrupts concurrent sessions, refreshing the sentinel timestamp, and gating all script-writing behind the Step-1 probe.

normalized: Near-final review asked for two probe additions and a corrected rationale for the acceptEdits-to-deny mapping (deny is right because ask is unreliable in that mode, not because no human is present), plus naming the resulting UX cost.

normalized: The plan's verification step used a failure mode (bad API key) that structurally cannot reach the timeout path it claims to verify, dropped an all-fail dedupe test case, retried on abort errors, and bundled a risky refactor with the correctness fix without a checkpoint.
verbatim: Must fix: The live "abort path" test doesn't test the abort path — same sin as last time. ... A bad key is an auth failure that rejects instantly; it never arms the deadline timer.

normalized: The plan's new abort guard keyed on err.name === "AbortError", but the installed SDK's abort error class never sets that name, so the guard would silently fall through to the retry it was meant to prevent.
verbatim: The abort-error identifier is wrong against this SDK. I checked the installed 0.102.0 — its user-abort error is APIUserAbortError ... never sets .name = "AbortError".

normalized: The plan's verify step could pass with zero tests discovered, an unused destructured binding would fail lint with certainty, route validation did not protect the five-distinct-lenses invariant, two stale ARCHITECTURE.md lines contradicted the design, and the standalone reviewers route was premature before any consumer existed.
verbatim: The test script may run zero tests and report green — the exact false-pass this whole plan exists to kill.

normalized: Verification specifics were still loose: one invariant test checked the input rather than the function under test, the expected test count was unstated, the rejection-sampling bound needed documenting, and a deferred validation requirement was not attached to the ROADMAP line where it would be picked up.
